records.ety: [F. admirer, fr. L. admirari; ad + mirari to wonder, for smirari, akin to Gr. <?/ to smile, Skr. smi, and E. smile.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Admired (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Admiring (<?/).]

defs: To regard with wonder or astonishment; to view with surprise; to marvel at.
quotes: Examples rather to be admired than imitated.

defs: To regard with wonder and delight; to look upon with an elevated feeling of pleasure, as something which calls out approbation, esteem, love, or reverence; to estimate or prize highly; as, to admire a person of high moral worth, to admire a landscape.
quotes: Admired as heroes and as gods obeyed.
notes: ☞ Admire followed by the infinitive is obsolete or colloquial; as, I admire to see a man consistent in his conduct.

defs: To wonder; to marvel; to be affected with surprise; -- sometimes with at.
quotes: To wonder at Pharaoh, and even admire at myself.
